title: IDACC Wiki Manifest
description: Compact app-readable navigation and source manifest. Human wiki content is being rebuilt as Markdown under docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.md.

editContract.format: Minimal JSON manifest for the desktop app. Keep prose short; put long-form docs in Markdown.

pages.purpose: Operator home: view the current routed team lead, talk to that lead, watch cross-team coordination, and read the live activity feed.
pages.scope: All-team observation with a read-only chat target. The embedded chat dispatches to the routed team lead; the activity feed merges events across teams.
pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the operator home for live fleet status, routed lead chat, and recent activity. Keep this page lightweight; detailed behavioral history belongs in release notes, not the live wiki manifest. The renderer store coalesces and version-stamps sync domains so page remounts observe the latest invalidation state without extra broad refreshes. Broad all-team roster polling is kept on a moderate cadence while the event stream keeps activity fresh.

pages.purpose: Operator decision queue: answer blockers surfaced by agents, Work Plans, Work Learn review gates, and manager inbox items waiting on a human reply, with store-level duplicate suppression.
pages.scope: All local blocker questions across teams plus manager inbox from the live store snapshot.
pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the review surface for blocker questions and approval prompts. It should stay focused on actionable pending decisions and their source context. Work plan actions can now surface deferred lead routing separately from hard failures, so review copy should preserve that distinction.

pages.purpose: Work operating surface: goals, plans, Learn material ingestion, live Kanban, recurring schedules, agent chains, dreams, and hidden context-budgeted dispatch.
pages.scope: All-team task view for the board; creation and assignment panels can target the selected team, selected teams, team leads, or every active agent depending on assignment scope.
pages.body:
# Work

Work owns goals, plans, tasks, Learn intake, schedules, loops, and dreams. Task creation and assignment should be explicit, manager-routed, and guarded against draft/replay duplication. Learn materials are auto-processed one at a time by the main process: queued rows drain in the background, material write/remove progress now publishes Learn sync changes while the tab is open, blocked rows remain only while blocking recommendations are still draft, and ready rows are hidden from the active queue. The Learn queue and review panes are constrained below intake so selected review content wraps inside its panel instead of crowding submission controls. Work fanout and decomposition defer instead of enqueueing more lead prompts when the target lead already has too many active queries. Shared sync-domain rules must keep read calls non-invalidating so Work-adjacent pages do not reload themselves in loops.

pages.purpose: Local project tracker with workspace discovery, metadata editing, GitHub import, git status, and git operation requests.
pages.scope: App-local settings store plus filesystem/git/GitHub helpers in the main process.
pages.body:
# Projects

Projects tracks local workspace repositories and reviewed project actions. It should describe current operator flows, not release history.

pages.purpose: HR Manager Health tab for fleet health, local-model token throughput, liveness probes, and the full fleet roster with harness/model/lifecycle controls, neutral provider model-lane visibility, and read-only lead markers. Hidden context-budget savings are backend-only and not displayed here.
pages.scope: Embedded in HR Manager between Structure and Build; the legacy health route opens this tab. Usage spans local-model providers and refreshes as its own observability stream; roster is holistic and grouped by team through AgentTable. Harness/model lane reads stay unsubscribed until an operator opens model lanes or focuses a runtime/model picker, then use runtime-catalog scoped caches across tab remounts so ordinary agent roster churn, settings edits, module refreshes, and page switches do not reload provider state.
pages.body:
# HR Manager Health

Health is the fleet observation and lifecycle surface inside HR Manager. It shows running state, runtime evidence, and guarded probe/lifecycle controls. Harness and model selectors use cached passive state by default; focusing a selector opens only a short-lived detail window, while the model-lanes panel pins runtime catalog details and cooldown polling. Passive runtime model, freshness, and subscription reads are held in the bridge cache for the same 5-minute window as subscription status, while the explicit re-check action remains the live refresh path for provider and CLI model evidence.

pages.purpose: Manage agent onchain identity, wallet controller address, Safe-style account, and scoped session keys.
pages.scope: All-team agent roster with wallet-bearing agents sorted first. Key provider is currently mock unless a live provider is wired behind the same interface.
pages.body:
# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys owns controller proof, wallet provisioning, and scoped key actions. Keep this page focused on current authority and safety boundaries. Shared API query lifecycle types may include active-query depth for routing guards; identity reads that only inspect settings should not broadcast page invalidations, create tasks, or dispatch work.

pages.purpose: Organization design, team building, roster lifecycle, selected-agent goals/instruction sidecars, hierarchy sync, secondary validation coverage, guarded team maintenance, and outbound relay topology.
pages.scope: All teams for graph/overview; active team for relay policy, hierarchy, and team operations; selected-agent instruction editing stays team-qualified from Structure.
pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager owns team structure, hierarchy, routing, relays, selected-agent instructions, and team lifecycle controls. It is the source for delegation topology. The Build tab caches runtime/provider/subscription data separately from library skills: runtime selectors refresh only on runtime-catalog mutations, while skills refresh on modules mutations. Normal roster churn, unrelated Settings writes, and module-only refreshes do not stall the harness dropdowns. Structure, Route, and Health stay off the Build catalog subscription, passive Build catalog reads are coalesced in the main bridge, and a pristine blank builder row adopts the cached default runtime as soon as the catalog arrives instead of waiting for a manual dropdown correction.

pages.purpose: Capability workbench for MCP tool servers, skills, and bundled plugins.
pages.scope: Active team, all teams, or all leads depending on scope selector. Runtime support is advisory; capability assignment remains runtime-neutral while stale target rows are still blocked by fresh-read guards.
pages.body:
# Capabilities

Capabilities owns skills, MCP servers, plugins, runtime-neutral capability wiring, and guarded Brain sync handoffs. Provider and runtime catalog reads stay cached/non-invalidating; provider connects, subscription account mutations, local model install/remove actions, and explicit runtime probes still refresh dependent views without passive CLI command execution. Dashboard draft/proposal reads are review-only; they do not auto-promote agent output into Tasks or dispatch assignments.

pages.purpose: Supervised local computer-control broker: bless capable agents, arm/disarm control, watch the screen, approve actions, and panic-stop.
pages.scope: Local machine. Requires macOS Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions; actions only run through the armed broker.
pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use owns the local broker, blessed-agent attachment, approval flow, and immediate stop controls for agents that can drive the Mac.

pages.purpose: Infrastructure panel for host hardware, manager connection, coordinator status with focused HR Manage handoff, self-update, subscriptions, local models, image server, local stacks, and inference backends.
pages.scope: Local app settings plus manager-facing provider/concurrency routes.
pages.body:
# Settings

Settings owns local manager connection, update status, provider/back-end configuration, local model setup, subscription sign-ins, and runtime availability evidence. Passive account-status reads stay cached and non-invalidating without warming model catalogs on open/focus/timer; account sign-in/sign-out/install, provider sync, explicit runtime probe, and local model pull/remove/catalog actions publish runtime-catalog changes so HR runtime selectors refresh only when those inputs actually change. Other settings writes stay on the settings domain and do not invalidate HR harness dropdown caches, and ordinary runtime/status reads reuse bridge cache for up to 5 minutes.

pages.purpose: Live documentation and editable UI schematic for the Control Center itself.
pages.scope: Reads this JSON file through app-local IPC. Does not touch manager state.
pages.body:
# Wiki

This page now reads a compact app manifest. The replacement authoring contract is being rebuilt in docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.md as Markdown-first schema documentation. The renderer loads and polls the wiki manifest only while the Wiki page is open, keeping the rest of the app free from idle wiki refresh work.
